theme: decode a scrambled sensor's calibration curve
title: Scrambled Sensor: Recover a Sparse Calibration Law Through a Hidden Affine Change of Variable
strategies: constant baseline: emit the mean of the training targets (no structure, ~0.1)
quadratic (low-order) least-squares fit in the raw reading x: fits the narrow window, wrong shape, extrapolates poorly -> the obvious-recipe trap

affine-conjugation sparse search: grid the hidden (a,b), pull data back to u=a*x+b, select the sparsest t-term polynomial whose train residual hits the noise floor -> recovers the law and extrapolates
headroom above strong: finer (a,b) grid / continuous refinement / integer-coefficient snapping drives held-out error toward the irreducible noise floor
